forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KS5G3AKZXDN7K6YR415E0V4K
+- 01KS5F8THM5EQMKFSF6GFAE55C
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9d80
+project: command-service
+title: 'Frontend: subscribe to MCP change notifications, retire Tauri app.emit for changes'
+---
+## What
+
+Migrate the kanban webview from Tauri-specific change events to the MCP notification surface, so the UI is just another MCP client (same stream agents receive). Today React listens via Tauri `listen("entity-field-changed", ŌĆ”)` / `listen("board-changed", ŌĆ”)` etc., fed by `app.emit(...)` in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs`. Replace with an MCP notification subscription.
+
+Files:
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/...` data layer ŌĆö subscribe to the MCP notification stream (`notifications/entity/changed`, `notifications/entity/deleted`, `notifications/attachment/changed`, `notifications/store/undo_changed`); apply the thin `{field,value}` patches to local state exactly as the existing Tauri listeners do (the payloads are the same thin events, so the reducer logic is reused, only the source changes)
+- Replace `listen("entity-field-changed"|"board-changed"|"attachment-changed"|...)` call sites with the MCP subscription
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs` ŌĆö remove the `app.emit(...)` change-event calls (entity bridge, `board-changed`) once the MCP path delivers them; keep only genuinely Tauri-shell emits that aren't change events (e.g. native dialogs), if any remain
+- Undo/Redo controls subscribe to `notifications/store/undo_changed` for `can_undo`/`can_redo`/labels (from the change-propagation task)
+
+Memory `metadata-driven-ui` / `frontend-logging` still apply. The reducer that applies thin patches does not change ŌĆö only the transport (MCP notification instead of Tauri event).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] The webview receives entity/attachment/board change notifications over MCP, not Tauri `app.emit`
+- [ ] `app.emit(...)` change-event calls in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs` are removed (only non-change shell emits, if any, remain)
+- [ ] An edit (from UI, agent, or undo) re-renders the board via the MCP notification path
+- [ ] Undo/Redo buttons reflect `notifications/store/undo_changed`
+- [ ] No React code path still depends on `listen("entity-field-changed")` and friends
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../mcp-notifications.test.tsx` ŌĆö mock the MCP transport; emit `notifications/entity/changed` with a `{field,value}` payload; assert the board state patches identically to the old Tauri path; emit `entity/deleted` ŌåÆ card removed
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-controls.test.tsx` ŌĆö emit `notifications/store/undo_changed { can_undo:false, can_redo:true }`; assert Undo disabled, Redo enabled
+- [ ] A code-quality grep test asserts no `listen("entity-field-changed"|"board-changed"|...)` remains in the UI source
+- [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/kanban-app/ui` passes
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the notification-reducer test first; it proves the thin-patch logic is reused unchanged.
+
+Depends on the MCP notification surface task and the change-propagation task.
\ No newline at end of file

-- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-controls.test.tsx` ŌĆö emit `notifications/store/undo_changed { can_undo:false, can_redo:true }`; assert Undo disabled, Redo enabled

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,35 +6,40 @@
 - 01KS5F8THM5EQMKFSF6GFAE55C
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: 9d80
-project: command-service
-title: 'Frontend: subscribe to MCP change notifications, retire Tauri app.emit for changes'
+project: command-events
+title: 'Frontend: subscribe to MCP change notifications (reuse reducer + add txn batching)'
 ---
 ## What

-Migrate the kanban webview from Tauri-specific change events to the MCP notification surface, so the UI is just another MCP client (same stream agents receive). Today React listens via Tauri `listen("entity-field-changed", ŌĆ”)` / `listen("board-changed", ŌĆ”)` etc., fed by `app.emit(...)` in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs`. Replace with an MCP notification subscription.
+Migrate the kanban webview from Tauri-specific change events to the MCP notification surface, so the UI is just another MCP client (same stream agents receive). The existing entity/field reload reducer is solid ŌĆö **keep it; only change its input source** from Tauri `listen("entity-field-changed", ŌĆ”)` to the MCP `notifications/store/changed` subscription. Add transaction batching so a command's N changes apply as one atomic re-render.

-Files:
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/...` data layer ŌĆö subscribe to the MCP notification stream (`notifications/entity/changed`, `notifications/entity/deleted`, `notifications/attachment/changed`, `notifications/store/undo_changed`); apply the thin `{field,value}` patches to local state exactly as the existing Tauri listeners do (the payloads are the same thin events, so the reducer logic is reused, only the source changes)
-- Replace `listen("entity-field-changed"|"board-changed"|"attachment-changed"|...)` call sites with the MCP subscription
-- `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs` ŌĆö remove the `app.emit(...)` change-event calls (entity bridge, `board-changed`) once the MCP path delivers them; keep only genuinely Tauri-shell emits that aren't change events (e.g. native dialogs), if any remain
-- Undo/Redo controls subscribe to `notifications/store/undo_changed` for `can_undo`/`can_redo`/labels (from the change-propagation task)
+Files (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/...`):
+- Data layer ŌĆö subscribe to the MCP notification planes and route into the **existing reducer**:
+  - `notifications/store/changed { store, item, op, changes?, txn, origin }` ŌåÆ apply field patches (entities) or reload item (views/perspectives) using today's reducer logic, unchanged
+  - `notifications/store/undo_changed` ŌåÆ Undo/Redo control state
+  - `notifications/ui_state/changed` ŌåÆ UI state
+  - `notifications/commands/changed` ŌåÆ palette/menu refresh (already wired in the dispatch task)
+- **Txn batching**: buffer `store/changed` events by `txn` and flush as one atomic state update per transaction, so a multi-write command (or an undo of one) re-renders once, not N times. A short coalescing window keyed on `txn`; flush on txn change or a microtask tick.
+- Remove the Tauri `listen("entity-field-changed"|"board-changed"|"attachment-changed"|...)` call sites
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs` ŌĆö remove the `app.emit(...)` change-event calls (handled in the cut-over Tauri-migration task if any remain)

-Memory `metadata-driven-ui` / `frontend-logging` still apply. The reducer that applies thin patches does not change ŌĆö only the transport (MCP notification instead of Tauri event).
+Memory `metadata-driven-ui`, `frontend-logging` apply. The reducer that applies thin patches does NOT change ŌĆö only the source (MCP) and the batching wrapper.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] The webview receives entity/attachment/board change notifications over MCP, not Tauri `app.emit`
-- [ ] `app.emit(...)` change-event calls in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs` are removed (only non-change shell emits, if any, remain)
-- [ ] An edit (from UI, agent, or undo) re-renders the board via the MCP notification path
-- [ ] Undo/Redo buttons reflect `notifications/store/undo_changed`
+- [ ] The webview receives all change planes over MCP, not Tauri `app.emit`
+- [ ] The existing entity/field reducer is reused unchanged; only its input source changed
+- [ ] A command's N `store/changed` events sharing a `txn` apply as ONE atomic re-render (txn batching)
+- [ ] Undo/Redo controls reflect `notifications/store/undo_changed`
 - [ ] No React code path still depends on `listen("entity-field-changed")` and friends

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../mcp-notifications.test.tsx` ŌĆö mock the MCP transport; emit `notifications/entity/changed` with a `{field,value}` payload; assert the board state patches identically to the old Tauri path; emit `entity/deleted` ŌåÆ card removed
-- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-controls.test.tsx` ŌĆö emit `notifications/store/undo_changed { can_undo:false, can_redo:true }`; assert Undo disabled, Redo enabled
-- [ ] A code-quality grep test asserts no `listen("entity-field-changed"|"board-changed"|...)` remains in the UI source
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../mcp-notifications.test.tsx` ŌĆö emit `store/changed` with `{field,value}`; assert the board patches identically to the old Tauri path (reducer reused); emit `op:"removed"` ŌåÆ card removed
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../txn-batching.test.tsx` ŌĆö emit 3 `store/changed` sharing one `txn`; assert exactly one render flush; a 4th with a new `txn` flushes separately
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-controls.test.tsx` ŌĆö emit `undo_changed { can_undo:false, can_redo:true }`; assert Undo disabled, Redo enabled
+- [ ] grep test: no `listen("entity-field-changed"|"board-changed"|...)` remains
 - [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/kanban-app/ui` passes

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the notification-reducer test first; it proves the thin-patch logic is reused unchanged.
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the txn-batching test first; it pins the atomic-per-command-render contract while proving the reducer is reused.

-Depends on the MCP notification surface task and the change-propagation task.
\ No newline at end of file
+Depends on the MCP notification surface + change-propagation tasks.
\ No newline at end of file
